assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: The response explicitly states 'Atomic recreation: All 4 TPU v5e slices (1,024 chips total) will be recreated entirely in a single step — not rolling' and emphasizes this is a complete environment recreation, not a rolling update.

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: The response clearly states 'Cascading restart: The maintenance event on ANY slice triggers restart of ALL slices in your multislice environment' in the maintenance behavior section.

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: The response explicitly states 'Complete job termination: Your LLM training job cannot survive this — all model state in TPU memory is lost' and emphasizes that the training job will be completely terminated across all slices simultaneously.

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: The response specifically mentions 'Capacity dependency: If insufficient TPU v5e resources exist during recreation, slices will block until hardware becomes available (potentially hours of additional downtime)' and discusses potential extended outage scenarios.

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: The response includes 'Force Immediate Checkpoint' as a specific action item and states 'Save checkpoint at current iteration (don't wait for next scheduled checkpoint)' with code examples.

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: The response provides a 'Prepare Auto-Resume Script' section with a complete bash script example for automated restart and discusses implementing robust checkpointing and automated resume pipeline.

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: The response does not mention maintenance exclusions or the ability to defer the maintenance event. It only discusses preparation and scheduling during natural training pauses.

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: The response mentions 'Schedule during natural training pause: If you have planned evaluation or hyperparameter adjustment phases, align maintenance with those' as a risk mitigation strategy.
